description: Lands Awakened Patch 6 (Season 15 Patch) (2 March 2022) — [03. March 2022] Lands Awakened Hotfix 6.1, Controller Support Beta, Might and Favor Increased, Crystal League Changes, Randomized and Static Dungeon Changes, Automatic Silver Pickup, Party Marker Improvements, "Hideout Under Attack" State, Debuff Immunity Rework, Looting Dismount Changes, Other Changes and Improvements, Combat Balance Changes, Fixes.

sections.heading: Controller Support Beta

subsections.heading: Controller support
subsections.searchable_text: Controller support

subsections.heading: Steam Deck support

items.text: Currently, controllers are activated by typing a command into the in-game chat window. For the list of controller commands, see this forum post: forum.albiononline.com/index.p…ler-Support-Live-Testing/
For general updates on controller support, or to ask questions or give feedback, please use this dedicated subforum: forum.albiononline.com/index.p…d/171-Controller-Testing/

sections.heading: Might and Favor Increased

items.text: Might and Favor acquired through Gathering/Fishing (Outlands and Roads): + ~31%
Might and Favor acquired through PvE (Outlands and Roads): + ~23%
Might and Favor from Crystal Spiders: +5%
Might and Favor from Siphoning Mages: +10%
Might and Favor from winning in Corrupted Dungeons: +20%
Might and Favor from Hellgates: +15%

sections.heading: Randomized and Static Dungeon Changes

subsections.heading: Randomized Dungeons

items.text: This affects Randomized Dungeons of all tiers and zone types, and includes both Solo (green) and Group (blue) Dungeons
Dungeons that are in an elevated mob / rewards state are indicated by updated visuals on the dungeon entrance

subsections.searchable_text: Randomized Dungeons This affects Randomized Dungeons of all tiers and zone types, and includes both Solo (green) and Group (blue) Dungeons Dungeons that are in an elevated mob / rewards state are indicated by updated visuals on the dungeon entrance

subsections.heading: Static Dungeons

items.text: Guard Lieutenant, Commander of the Guard (Morgana)

subitems.text: Increased time between casts to allow for more auto-attacks

items.text: Cryomancer, Master Cryomancer (Undead)

subitems.text: Cooldown of Frostbolt 4s → 8s to allow for more auto-attacks

sections.heading: Automatic Silver Pickup

items.text: Silver is now, by default, picked up automatically. This can be disabled via a new option in General Settings. Details

subitems.text: Silver is not automatically picked up if it is protected, if the player is mounted, or if it would result in the termination of an effect that manual pickup would normally cause (e.g. invisibility or protection buffs acquired from changing regions)
Silver is automatically picked up immediately after these temporary effects expire
Silver can still manually be picked up, which results in the usual termination of temporary effects that are normally ended by world interaction

items.text: This replaces the pickup behavior previously available only on mobile, and should result in a much smoother experience on mobile devices

sections.searchable_text: Automatic Silver Pickup Silver is now, by default, picked up automatically. This can be disabled via a new option in General Settings. Details Silver is not automatically picked up if it is protected, if the player is mounted, or if it would result in the termination of an effect that manual pickup would normally cause (e.g. invisibility or protection buffs acquired from changing regions) Silver is automatically picked up immediately after these temporary effects expire Silver can still manually be picked up, which results in the usual termination of temporary effects that are normally ended by world interaction This replaces the pickup behavior previously available only on mobile, and should result in a much smoother experience on mobile devices

sections.heading: Party Marker Improvements

items.text: Party markers on non-party players now persist through zone changes, as other party markers have since Patch 4
Additionally, based on community feedback, party markers now remain on characters even after death

sections.searchable_text: Party Marker Improvements Party markers on non-party players now persist through zone changes, as other party markers have since Patch 4 Additionally, based on community feedback, party markers now remain on characters even after death

sections.heading: "Hideout Under Attack" State

items.text: Improved feedback for characters inside Hideouts that are currently under attack: a musical and ambience change now accompanies the attack messages, along with visual effects that better communicate the outside state while the Hideout is actively being damaged

sections.searchable_text: "Hideout Under Attack" State Improved feedback for characters inside Hideouts that are currently under attack: a musical and ambience change now accompanies the attack messages, along with visual effects that better communicate the outside state while the Hideout is actively being damaged

sections.heading: Debuff Immunity Rework

subsections.heading: immunity to certain debuffs

items.text: When using a portal invisibility shrine after being force-dismounted, the immunity to Silence was canceling the Silence aspect of the shrine. Since shrines are triggered by the player, the Silence effect now ignores the immunity.

When the Smart Cluster Queue activated after a forced dismount, the Stun effect of being moved to a different zone was ignored. The Smart Cluster Queue Stun effect now always applies to players moved via this system.

subsections.searchable_text: immunity to certain debuffs When using a portal invisibility shrine after being force-dismounted, the immunity to Silence was canceling the Silence aspect of the shrine. Since shrines are triggered by the player, the Silence effect now ignores the immunity. When the Smart Cluster Queue activated after a forced dismount, the Stun effect of being moved to a different zone was ignored. The Smart Cluster Queue Stun effect now always applies to players moved via this system.

sections.searchable_text: Debuff Immunity Rework immunity to certain debuffs When using a portal invisibility shrine after being force-dismounted, the immunity to Silence was canceling the Silence aspect of the shrine. Since shrines are triggered by the player, the Silence effect now ignores the immunity. When the Smart Cluster Queue activated after a forced dismount, the Stun effect of being moved to a different zone was ignored. The Smart Cluster Queue Stun effect now always applies to players moved via this system.

sections.heading: Looting Dismount Changes

subsections.heading: dismount

items.text: To require a greater commitment to potential PvP while looting in combat situations, bringing greater risk to a previously easy reward
To prevent players from accidentally losing their shield bubble when clicking on a corpse, which is often used to bait less experienced players into being easily attackable

subsections.searchable_text: dismount To require a greater commitment to potential PvP while looting in combat situations, bringing greater risk to a previously easy reward To prevent players from accidentally losing their shield bubble when clicking on a corpse, which is often used to bait less experienced players into being easily attackable

sections.searchable_text: Looting Dismount Changes dismount To require a greater commitment to potential PvP while looting in combat situations, bringing greater risk to a previously easy reward To prevent players from accidentally losing their shield bubble when clicking on a corpse, which is often used to bait less experienced players into being easily attackable

sections.heading: Other Changes and Improvements

items.text: Added option to create Loadouts while inspecting another player and via Kill / Death Screen details
Treasure Mobs (i.e. animals with 30 gatherable Hide charges, added with the Lands Awakened update) now remain on the minimap until fully harvested – previously their markers disappeared upon death
Rare fish of all types are no longer biome-exclusive, and now have a small chance to be caught in other select biomes
Added additional info to Marketplace Sell Order notifications when items sell above their original asking price
Updated knockback behavior of Energy Vortices to feel more responsive and reactive
Improved behavior of rotatable channeling spells in situations with worse ping
Opening treasure chests now always requires a channel, even if the chest has already been opened by another player
Crescent Slash (Double-Bladed Staff) has been renamed to Soaring Swipe to distinguish it from the E ability of Clarent Blade
The Earthkeeper Shaman in Group Randomized Dungeons now marks possible targets of her melee attack

sections.heading: Combat Balance Changes

subsections.heading: Arcane Staffs

items.text: Mimic (all Arcane Staffs)

subsections.searchable_text: Arcane Staffs Mimic (all Arcane Staffs) Timeout Cooldown 20s → 0s

subsections.heading: Axes

items.text: Blood Bandit (Battleaxe)

subitems.text: Can now be used twice in a row
First use deals physical damage depending on the Rending Bleed Charges on the target and immediately resets the cooldown.

Second use deals 110 physical damage and heals the caster based on the Rending Bleed Charges of the first enemy hit. (This heal only applies for the first enemy hit; consecutive hits do not grant additional healing).
Time Window for the second activation 10s

subsections.searchable_text: Axes Blood Bandit (Battleaxe) Can now be used twice in a row First use deals physical damage depending on the Rending Bleed Charges on the target and immediately resets the cooldown. Second use deals 110 physical damage and heals the caster based on the Rending Bleed Charges of the first enemy hit. (This heal only applies for the first enemy hit; consecutive hits do not grant additional healing). Time Window for the second activation 10s Cooldown: 10s → 20s

subsections.heading: Hammers

items.text: Giant Steps (Forge Hammers)

subitems.text: While Giant Steps is active, the caster can use the new ability Giant Smash
Effect: Slam hammers on the ground, dealing 75 physical damage in a 4m radius
The ability can be used repeatedly, as long as Giant Steps is active

subsections.searchable_text: Hammers Giant Steps (Forge Hammers) Removed Attack Damage Buff While Giant Steps is active, the caster can use the new ability Giant Smash

sections.heading: Fixes

items.text: Fixed an issue where dead players would trigger Hostile Dismount status
Fixed issue where melee attacks could hit after attacker death
Fixed an issue where Sell Order Completed notifications always showed the original sell order value, even if the items sold for a higher price
Fixed a rare issue in Hellgate party creation which could lead to players in a party still being attackable to each other
Removed Max HP Buff from Stonestream Lurcher as it was incorrectly overlapping with the buff of Stonestream Lurcher Sandwich – the Sandwich now correctly adds the Max HP Buff on top
Numerous additional graphical, animation, terrain, UI, audio, and localization fixes

sections.searchable_text: Fixes Fixed an issue where dead players would trigger Hostile Dismount status Fixed issue where melee attacks could hit after attacker death Fixed an issue where Sell Order Completed notifications always showed the original sell order value, even if the items sold for a higher price Fixed a rare issue in Hellgate party creation which could lead to players in a party still being attackable to each other Removed Max HP Buff from Stonestream Lurcher as it was incorrectly overlapping with the buff of Stonestream Lurcher Sandwich – the Sandwich now correctly adds the Max HP Buff on top Numerous additional graphical, animation, terrain, UI, audio, and localization fixes
